source: NvChad - Install Neovim: Neovim's origin, features and the built-in tutorial

options: a fork of the Vim editor
Mainly because Vim lacked asynchronous job support at the time
questions.why: Async job support was the main early motivation.

questions.q: Which language did Neovim choose for embedding/configuration?
questions.why: Lua (via LuaJIT) is fast and easy to embed.

questions.q: From which Neovim version are Treesitter and LSP support included?
questions.why: Since version 0.5, reducing the plugins needed for advanced editing.

questions.q: A key strength of Neovim is that its configuration...
options: is contained in a single file that can be version-controlled and synced across installs
is stored in a binary format
must be edited in a GUI
questions.why: One config file distributes cleanly via git across machines.

questions.q: Which command opens the interactive tutorial for learning Neovim?
questions.why: :Tutor opens a file you can read and practice in.

questions.q: Compared with Vim, Neovim's development model is...
questions.why: Neovim has more open community development than Vim.
